title: oxalis monophylla var. stenophylla
intro: Oxalis monophylla var. stenophylla, also known as the narrow-leaved wood-sorrel, is a type of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant. The health benefits of Oxalis monophylla var. stenophylla include its potential to reduce blood pressure, improve kidney function, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies due to its high concentration of oxalic acid, which has been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties.
